summary: Sprint 4 (Client Data & Asset Management) backend tested end-to-end: 31/31 pytest cases passed. Covered stats, client list with filters, detail aggregation (assets + monitoring + tickets), monitoring upsert with status normalisation, full ticket CRUD with status pipeline + auto-assignment + attachment-append + timestamps, CSV export, multi-tenant isolation (with a freshly registered secondary tenant), and Sprint 2 + Sprint 3 smoke. No bugs found in the implementation.

minor.issue: pydantic EmailStr rejects .test TLD (special-use). Not a Sprint 4 bug but worth noting since dev/test fixtures often use .test/.local. Real users on .com/.in unaffected.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1605 lines — strongly consider splitting Sprint 4 endpoints into a router module (e.g. backend/routes/client_data.py + routes/service_tickets.py). Single-file approach is becoming hard to navigate.

list_client_data filters status AFTER the DB query in Python (acknowledged in spec). Fine for current scale, but at >2000 handover clients this will become a bottleneck. Future: store the latest inverter_status snapshot on the client doc or use a lookup-aggregate pipeline.

update_ticket builds timeline_entry incrementally and only the LAST mutation determines `action` (e.g. setting status+priority+assigned_to in one PATCH yields only one ambiguous entry). Consider emitting one timeline event per field change.

CSV export currently calls list_client_data(user=user) as a Python coroutine — it is awaited via FastAPI's normal route call, which works, but the in-process call bypasses any Depends/Query validation. Acceptable here but worth a comment.
Monitoring `password` is stored in plaintext in inverter_monitoring docs (per spec). For production, consider envelope-encryption at rest.
create_ticket uses count_documents to generate next ticket number — race-prone under concurrent creates. A small atomic counter (findOneAndUpdate $inc on a counters collection) would be safer.
PATCH update_ticket: when status auto-bumps to 'Assigned' because of an assignment, the timeline only records the assignment action (no separate Status→Assigned entry). Minor UX nit.
test_credentials: bulk-test@solarix.com / Test@1234 (primary). Secondary tenant registered on the fly per test session under test_sprint4_<uuid>@solarix-tests.com.
seed_data_creation: Created one TEST_NotHandover_* lead client + one TEST_ ticket with attachments, plus monitoring rows on the Handover Complete client. Secondary tenant 'TEST_Sprint4_Tenant_*' created per session. All TEST_-prefixed; no production data modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 4 backend is solid (31/31 green). The primary tenant's first Handover Complete client (Ramesh Kumar Patil) now has monitoring row inverter_status='Online' and at least one Closed ticket plus one open TEST attach ticket. CSV export header order is locked — any FE change must keep parsing consistent. Multi-tenant isolation verified at every Sprint 4 endpoint. Sprint 2 (ai-extract) and Sprint 3 (document-templates list) smoke also green.
